data.summary: Core reveal discipline is strong: the Soviet first shot, Charlotte damage, Beckwith’s defensive torpedo, debris projection, Reyes maintenance plant, and hotel knowledge lag are all present. Publication should pause for several concrete continuity/contract fixes, especially word band, clock/range mechanics, current direction, and the final POV access leak. Deterministic validators also reported blocking issues.

issues.title: Chapter is one word below required word band
issues.description: Deterministic validator reports 3699 words against the required 3700–4700 band.
issues.fixInstruction: After the other edits, rerun word count and ensure the chapter is at least 3700 words and no more than 4700.

issues.title: Zulu timestamps conflict with the fixed T+1:30 engagement anchor
issues.description: The prose dates Beckwith’s report at 14 SEP 79, 2318Z and logs the Soviet launch at 2348Z. The continuity anchor fixes the opening dinner at 19:00 local and the submarine clash/debris event at T+1:30, so these Zulu times place the clash too early or even before dinner depending on EST/EDT interpretation.
issues.fixInstruction: Retimestamp the Navy log sequence to align with T+1:30 relative to the opening dinner, including date rollover if needed. If using September Hatteras daylight time, the engagement should fall around 0030Z on 15 SEP; if using the bible’s strict EST, around 0130Z.

issues.title: Charlotte’s stated distance from Aurelia conflicts with final range readback
issues.description: The opening places Charlotte three nautical miles from Aurelia, but the ending has Cole identify Aurelia at bearing 258, range 1400. Standard sonar/chart range here reads as yards, making Aurelia far closer than three nautical miles. This also affects whether debris can plausibly reach the hotel next chapter.
issues.fixInstruction: Either change the opening distance to roughly match the 1400-yard chart geometry while remaining outside the 500-yard standoff, or revise all bearing/range calls and drift timing to support a true three-nautical-mile separation.

issues.title: Current set direction contradicts the debris drift direction
issues.description: Ennis says the set is 110 true, which in nautical usage means flow toward east-southeast, but the chapter repeatedly plots drift southwest toward bearings 258–263 and Aurelia.
issues.fixInstruction: Change the set to a southwest/west-southwest bearing consistent with the plotted debris path, or phrase it as current “from 110 true” if the intended flow is toward the southwest.

issues.title: Final scene breaks Beckwith’s close-third visual access to Cole
issues.description: Beckwith is hearing Cole over a handset, but the narration gives present visual details of Cole’s collar and face under red lamps. Unless Beckwith can physically see sonar, this violates the chapter’s single-POV discipline.
issues.fixInstruction: Establish line of sight to Cole before those details, or convert them into Beckwith’s earlier memory/inference, or remove the visual description.

issues.title: Several deterministic knowledge/entity flags appear to be false positives
issues.description: Aurelia, Triton, Daphne, and Nereid have prior chapter/packet support; Cole’s chart readback is required by the ending hook; Ennis does not actually know the later cover-up order in prose.
issues.fixInstruction: Update validator state/whitelisting rather than changing compliant prose, unless the build requires adding explicit metadata support markers.

issues.title: WORD_BAND
issues.description: Chapter word count 3699 falls outside target band 3700-4700.
issues.fixInstruction: Resolve the issue using this evidence: 3699

issues.title: KNOWLEDGE_BOUNDARY
issues.description: Petty Officer Cole may have crossed a stated knowledge boundary.
issues.fixInstruction: Resolve the issue using this evidence: Knows only what sonar tells him; uses the plain word for what he hears even when the bridge would prefer he not. | Knows through sonar that Sierra Three launched, Charlotte's weapon ran normally, Sierra Three broke up, and hard returns are drifting; by reading the chart he learns the square in that bearing/range is Aurelia, a commercial undersea installation beginning operations this date.

issues.fixInstruction: Resolve the deterministic validator failure without changing correct chapter facts.

issues.title: KNOWLEDGE_LEAK_PROSE
issues.description: Lt. Cmdr. Ennis appears near forbidden knowledge "Knows what Charlotte fired and at whom; knows what the Navy wishes the civilian record to say about it; chooses silence over dispute when his commander refuses an order." in prose.
issues.fixInstruction: Resolve the issue using this evidence: Lt. Cmdr. Ennis | Knows what Charlotte fired and at whom; knows what the Navy wishes the civilian record to say about it; chooses silence over dispute when his commander refuses an order.

issues.description: Petty Officer Cole appears near forbidden knowledge "Knows only what sonar tells him; uses the plain word for what he hears even when the bridge would prefer he not." in prose.

issues.title: KNOWLEDGE_LEAK_PROSE

issues.fixInstruction: Resolve the issue using this evidence: Soviet Captain | Charlotte hears him through radio fragments only; never makes contact with the Aurelia.
